{"content": "     1|# Skill — company-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`company-wiki` is the top-level platform-agnostic company knowledge surface.
     6|
     7|It owns:
     8|- one canonical Hermes-style root under `workspace/company-wiki/`
     9|- Slack + Trello source ingestion into one shared substrate
    10|- deduped raw/manifests under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    11|- derived wiki generation under sectioned directories (`entities/`, `concepts/`, `comparisons/`, `queries/`)
    12|- one unified local query surface
    13|- authoritative `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, `log.md` at the canonical root
    14|
    15|Use this when the user wants:
    16|- one company wiki/search surface instead of Slack/Trello silos
    17|- raw snapshot caching plus AI-generated wiki refresh
    18|- a cron/manual rebuild path for the company wiki
    19|- source-specific retrieval methods documented as one operating workflow
    20|
    21|## Retrieval Methodology
    22|
    23|The company wiki is a **4-stage pipeline**:
    24|
    25|1. **Retrieve** — pull whatever each source can actually expose right now
    26|2. **Persist raw** — save retrievable source payloads under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`
    27|3. **Normalize** — convert source-specific payloads into one shared derived-entry schema
    28|4. **Build/query** — generate/search derived wiki pages under `workspace/company-wiki/`
    29|
    30|Default retrieval/search always targets the **derived wiki**, not raw payloads.
    31|
    32|## Canonical root contract
    33|
    34|`company-wiki` is governed by the canonical root itself:
    35|- `workspace/company-wiki/SCHEMA.md`
    36|- `workspace/company-wiki/index.md`
    37|- `workspace/company-wiki/log.md`
    38|
    39|Reference-only Hermes material may remain under `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/`, but it is not authoritative for active company-wiki flow.
    40|
    41|## Source Adapters
    42|
    43|### Slack adapter
    44|
    45|Current retrieval tiers:
    46|
    47|1. **Workspace public-channel metadata**
    48|   - Uses `conversations.list`
    49|   - Captures public channel inventory, membership flags, and channel `updated` timestamps
    50|   - This is the baseline guaranteed path in this workspace
    51|
    52|2. **Public-channel message history raw**
    53|   - Uses `conversations.history` via the configured bot-token path after public-channel join
    54|   - Current refresh path attempts joins on selected public channels and persists raw history snapshots for channels where access succeeds
    55|   - Initial archive baseline runs use `--archive-mode archive` and persist history in chunked raw records plus per-channel checkpoints for resume
    56|   - Archive mode now also probes broader Slack inventory surfaces (`oversight.conversations.list`, `admin.conversations.search`) **if available** and widens candidate channels when those scopes actually work
    57|   - Hourly cron stays `incremental` only and uses per-channel latest message watermarks rather than replaying baseline pages
    58|   - This is the preferred path when the goal is a real content-backed company wiki refresh rather than metadata-only bootstrap
    59|   - Coverage is still partial and capability-dependent per channel/scope, so manifests must record successes and blockers explicitly
    60|
    61|3. **Private/admin inventory**
    62|   - Only allowed when the exact API path/scopes are verified
    63|   - Must never be implied from CLI login alone
    64|
    65|Excluded:
    66|- DM / 1:1
    67|- any unverified private-channel message visibility
    68|
    69|### Trello adapter
    70|
    71|Current retrieval order:
    72|
    73|1. `members/me` — auth/identity sanity check
    74|2. `members/me/organizations` — visible workspace/org list
    75|3. `members/me/boards` — visible board inventory
    76|4. `boards/<id>` — board detail
    77|5. `boards/<id>/members` — board member coverage
    78|6. `boards/<id>/cards` — board card snapshot
    79|
    80|Trello remains strictly **read-only**.
    81|
    82|## Raw Snapshot Contract
    83|
    84|Raw payloads live under:
    85|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/`
    86|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/trello/`
    87|
    88|Each source keeps:
    89|- `items/` — deduped raw payload records
    90|- `dedupe-index.json` — payload fingerprint index
    91|- `runs/manifest-*.json` — per-run source manifest
    92|- `channel-checkpoints/*.json` — per-channel archive/incremental resume state for Slack history capture
    93|
    94|Top-level run manifests live under:
    95|- `workspace/company-wiki/raw/run-manifests/`
    96|
    97|Rules:
    98|- raw is source-labeled
    99|- raw is snapshot-based, not event-sourced
   100|- repeated identical payloads should dedupe rather than multiply
   101|- Slack history payloads are persisted as chunked `channel_history_chunk` raw items rather than one large per-channel blob
   102|- raw may be incomplete if source capability is incomplete; blockers must be recorded explicitly
   103|
   104|## Normalize Contract
   105|
   106|Normalized entries are derived from raw snapshots and must preserve:
   107|- `source_type`
   108|- `access_scope`
   109|- `source_refs`
   110|- `raw_manifest_ref`
   111|- factual summary fields (`facts`, `decisions`, `actions`, `open_questions`, etc.)
   112|
   113|What normalization does:
   114|- maps source-specific payloads into one shared wiki-entry shape
   115|- preserves provenance and caveats
   116|- avoids leaking raw bodies into derived page content
   117|
   118|## Query Semantics
   119|
   120|Default query path:
   121|
   122|```bash
   123|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   124|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   125|  --query "product risk"
   126|```
   127|
   128|Query semantics:
   129|- searches Hermes-style derived sections:
   130|  - `entities/`
   131|  - `concepts/`
   132|  - `comparisons/`
   133|  - `queries/`
   134|- does not search raw payload bodies by default
   135|- returns mixed-source results in one surface
   136|- source provenance is preserved inside the page content/refs
   137|
   138|Interpretation rules:
   139|- a Slack page reflects what was retrievable from Slack at refresh time
   140|- a Trello page reflects current-state Trello snapshots at refresh time
   141|- mixed-source hits should be read as one company-memory surface, not as identical-confidence evidence
   142|
   143|## Refresh Runbook
   144|
   145|### Prepare Slack bot membership when richer history retrieval is needed
   146|
   147|```bash
   148|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   149|```
   150|
   151|### Full refresh
   152|
   153|```bash
   154|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py --slack-team moveis
   155|```
   156|
   157|### Initial public-channel archive baseline
   158|
   159|```bash
   160|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   161|  --slack-team moveis \
   162|  --archive-mode archive \
   163|  --slack-channel-limit 50 \
   164|  --slack-history-limit 200 \
   165|  --slack-history-chunk-size 100
   166|```
   167|
   168|This mode:
   169|1. joins the current high-priority public channels visible to the bot token
   170|2. probes broader workspace/admin inventory if those scopes are actually available
   171|3. captures as much channel history as the current access path can actually read
   172|4. stores history as chunked raw records
   173|4. writes/updates `raw/slack/channel-checkpoints/*.json`
   174|5. resumes incomplete baseline channels on later archive runs via saved Slack cursors
   175|
   176|Honest scope note:
   177|- if admin/oversight scopes are unavailable, archive mode falls back to the public-channel, bot-accessible path and records that limitation explicitly in raw manifests
   178|
   179|### Hourly incremental refresh (cron-safe default)
   180|
   181|```bash
   182|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py \
   183|  --slack-team moveis \
   184|  --archive-mode incremental
   185|```
   186|
   187|What it does:
   188|1. captures accessible Slack source payloads
   189|2. captures accessible Trello source payloads
   190|3. writes raw source manifests
   191|4. writes a top-level run manifest
   192|5. rebuilds derived wiki pages
   193|
   194|Incremental mode uses per-channel latest-message checkpoints/watermarks and remains the only mode wired into the hourly cron wrapper by default.
   195|
   196|### Query after refresh
   197|
   198|```bash
   199|python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   200|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   201|  --query "slack trello dev"
   202|```
   203|
   204|### Cron/manual wrapper
   205|
   206|```bash
   207|workspace/bin/run_company_wiki_refresh.sh
   208|```
   209|
   210|Operational reality in this workspace:
   211|- The canonical wiki root is `workspace/company-wiki/`, but the current wrapper scripts are still anchored to `ROOT="/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw"`.
   212|- Treat this as a migration-era layout: Hermes-style wiki structure on top of a ZeroClaw-rooted runtime path.
   213|- When auditing readiness, check both Hermes cron state and system crontab state; an empty Hermes cron list does not mean the refresh is unscheduled.
   214|
   215|Cron invariant:
   216|- `workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` installs the refresh into system crontab in `incremental` mode by default, currently at minute 17 of every hour unless overridden by `COMPANY_WIKI_CRON_SCHEDULE`.
   217|- Use `COMPANY_WIKI_ARCHIVE_MODE=archive` only for an intentional one-off/manual baseline catch-up, not the hourly cron loop.
   218|- Invoke the installer as a shell script (`bash workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh` or direct execute). Do not run `.sh` installers with `python3`.
   219|
   220|Optional cron installers:
   221|
   222|```bash
   223|workspace/bin/install_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
   224|workspace/bin/uninstall_company_wiki_refresh_cron.sh
   225|```
   226|
   227|## Failure Modes / Degrade Strategy
   228|
   229|- If one source is blocked, the refresh should still complete for other sources.
   230|- The blocked source must be marked in source manifests and the top-level run manifest.
   231|- Missing Slack history scope is a **capability limit**, not a hard failure.
   232|- Missing Trello credentials is a real blocker and must surface clearly.
   233|
   234|## Verification Checklist
   235|
   236|Minimum acceptable verification after meaningful changes:
   237|
   238|1. `python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/refresh.py --slack-team moveis`
   239|2. `python3 workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_query.py --root workspace/company-wiki --query "..."`
   240|3. `python3 -m unittest workspace/test/company_wiki_pipeline_test.py workspace/test/slack_org_wiki_guardrails_test.py`
   241|4. `node --test workspace/test/slack_org_wiki_build.test.js`
   242|5. live capability checks when relevant:
   243|   - Slack `conversations.list`
   244|   - Slack `conversations.history` probe
   245|   - Trello `/members/me`
   246|   - Trello `/members/me/boards`
   247|
   248|## Output Contract
   249|
   250|1. **Source refresh status**
   251|2. **Run manifest path**
   252|3. **Derived wiki build status**
   253|4. **Query findings**
   254|5. **Blocked-source caveats**
   255|
   256|## Guardrails
   257|
   258|- Raw source data may be persisted only under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/`.
   259|- Default wiki retrieval must return derived pages, not raw payloads.
   260|- Do not imply private-channel or DM visibility without verified access.
   261|- Do not imply Trello write authority.
   262|- Do not treat Slack metadata access as message-history access.
   263|", "total_lines": 262, "file_size": 9992, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — slack-org-wiki
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`slack-org-wiki` is the workspace-local Slack source lane for **official Slack CLI auth**, **Enterprise admin conversation inventory**, and **Slack ingestion into local wiki surfaces**.
     6|
     7|Use it when the user wants to:
     8|- bootstrap Slack admin access via the official Slack CLI
     9|- inspect org-wide accessible channels/conversations
    10|- turn recent Slack findings into an LLM-maintained company wiki
    11|- keep the current bot runtime while adding a separate admin/control lane
    12|
    13|## When to Use
    14|
    15|Route here for prompts like:
    16|- “Log me into Slack CLI and use my admin access.”
    17|- “Inventory every accessible Slack channel for this org.”
    18|- “Turn recent Slack chatter into an LLM wiki.”
    19|- “Use the official Slack CLI path, not pasted tokens.”
    20|- “Build a company memory/wiki from Slack without storing raw messages.”
    21|
    22|If the request is only about querying already-built wiki pages, prefer `company-wiki` or `slack-llm-wiki-retrieval`, both of which now route to the canonical `workspace/company-wiki/` surface.
    23|
    24|## Core Rules
    25|
    26|- Default to the assistant's configured Slack token/permissions when describing or executing Slack access in this workspace.
    27|- Treat Slack CLI user-auth as a deprecated/legacy path, not the default operating story.
    28|- Preserve the existing Socket Mode bot/runtime.
    29|- Do not paste auth tickets, challenge codes, or tokens into chat.
    30|- Do not put raw Slack transcripts, raw message bodies, or raw file bodies into derived wiki pages.
    31|- Raw Slack snapshots are allowed only under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` when using the unified `company-wiki` pipeline.
    32|- Distinguish clearly between:
    33|  1. CLI auth/bootstrap
    34|  2. conversation metadata inventory
    35|  3. ephemeral search/review
    36|  4. persistent LLM-wiki storage
    37|- Do not claim private-channel message visibility unless the specific Slack API path and permissions are actually available.
    38|- Treat Real-time Search results as non-persistable.
    39|- Require proper internal disclosure/consent/privacy review before storing derived Slack knowledge.
    40|- Do not position this workflow as covert surveillance; align it with legitimate admin/compliance/internal-knowledge use.
    41|
    42|## Local Helper Scripts
    43|
    44|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py`
    45|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py`
    46|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py`
    47|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py`
    48|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py`
    49|- `workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py`
    50|
    51|Reference docs:
    52|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/official-notes.md`
    53|- `workspace/docs/slack-org-wiki/llm-wiki-schema.md`
    54|
    55|## Workflows
    56|
    57|### 1. Confirm local Slack CLI/auth state
    58|
    59|```bash
    60|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/auth_status.py --format markdown
    61|```
    62|
    63|Use this first to confirm:
    64|- `slack` exists on PATH
    65|- local CLI version
    66|- `slack auth list` status
    67|- whether `~/.slack/credentials.json` exists
    68|- whether CLI credential entries and/or a service/admin token env var are present
    69|
    70|### 2. Inventory accessible conversations (metadata only)
    71|
    72|```bash
    73|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    74|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
    75|  --method conversations-list \
    76|  --channel-type public_channel \
    77|  --exclude-archived \
    78|  --format markdown
    79|```
    80|
    81|for the current directly accessible public-channel surface, or:
    82|
    83|```bash
    84|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    85|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
    86|  --method oversight \
    87|  --format markdown
    88|```
    89|
    90|or, with an explicit token:
    91|
    92|```bash
    93|SLACK_ADMIN_TOKEN=*** \
    94|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/conversation_catalog.py \
    95|  --method oversight \
    96|  --format markdown
    97|```
    98|
    99|Use this for org/workspace conversation cataloging. Output is metadata-only.
   100|
   101|### 3. Join visible public channels with the oracle bot when history access is needed
   102|
   103|```bash
   104|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/join_public_channels.py --all-public
   105|```
   106|
   107|Use this when the bot token has history scopes but is not yet a member of target public channels. Joining may convert `not_in_channel` into usable history access.
   108|
   109|### 4. Build Slack-derived pages into the canonical company wiki
   110|
   111|```bash
   112|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_build.py \
   113|  --input workspace/tmp/slack-summary.json \
   114|  --root workspace/company-wiki
   115|```
   116|
   117|Input must already be a **structured summary artifact**. Do not feed raw transcripts.
   118|
   119|### 5. Pull active public-channel candidates for manual/LLM summarization
   120|
   121|```bash
   122|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/recent_public_channels.py \
   123|  --credential-team <TEAM_ID_OR_DOMAIN> \
   124|  --days 7 \
   125|  --format markdown
   126|```
   127|
   128|This is a **channel-activity heuristic** based on channel metadata, not proof of recent message reads.
   129|
   130|### 6. Query the canonical company wiki
   131|
   132|```bash
   133|python3 workspace/bin/slack-org-wiki/wiki_query.py \
   134|  --root workspace/company-wiki \
   135|  --query "product risk"
   136|```
   137|
   138|## Output Contract
   139|
   140|Default structure:
   141|1. **Auth / Access State**
   142|2. **Inventory Findings**
   143|3. **Wiki Findings**
   144|4. **Caveats / Permission Limits**
   145|5. **Next Actions**
   146|
   147|## Guardrails
   148|
   149|- Never store raw Slack messages in generated wiki artifacts.
   150|- If raw Slack data is persisted for the unified company-wiki pipeline, keep it under `workspace/company-wiki/raw/slack/` with run-manifest visibility.
   151|- Slack wiki build/query wrappers are canonical-root adapters; they must not create a parallel Slack-only wiki root.
   152|- Never imply that Slack CLI auth alone grants unrestricted private-channel message access.
   153|- Prefer metadata inventory plus structured summaries over bulk collection.
   154|- Current public-channel “active in last 7 days” flow is heuristic unless `channels:history` or `search:read` is added later.
   155|- If the required admin/oversight scopes are missing, say so explicitly.
   156|", "total_lines": 155, "file_size": 5793, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — github-org-intel
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`github-org-intel` is the workspace-local GitHub portfolio intelligence surface.
     6|Use it when the user wants **read-only `gh` CLI analysis** of the internal org,
     7|repo/module health, cautious contributor/account signals, or management action
     8|items derived from GitHub state.
     9|
    10|This is a **workspace skill**, not a global Codex skill.
    11|
    12|## When to Use
    13|
    14|Route here for prompts like:
    15|
    16|- “Give me a GitHub portfolio snapshot for orbisoptimus.”
    17|- “Which repos are active vs stale right now?”
    18|- “Analyze `orbisoptimus/orbi_app` and tell me the repo health.”
    19|- “Give me a cautious contributor summary for bellman-move.”
    20|- “Turn current GitHub state into action items for product, engineering, and AI.”
    21|
    22|If the question is really about Orbi community/user behavior, route to Orbi
    23|surfaces first. Use this skill for **software portfolio / delivery / backlog /
    24|branch hygiene / contributor-signal** questions.
    25|
    26|## Core Rules
    27|
    28|- `gh` CLI first.
    29|- Read-only only.
    30|- No GitHub write actions.
    31|- No HR/performance scoring from GitHub traces.
    32|- Account analysis is opt-in and must include confidence + visibility caveats.
    33|- Prefer repo/stream-level judgment over person-level inference.
    34|- If auth or visibility is incomplete, say so explicitly.
    35|
    36|## Local Helper Scripts
    37|
    38|Use these workspace-local helpers:
    39|
    40|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py`
    41|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/repo_deep_dive.py`
    42|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/account_summary.py`
    43|- `workspace/bin/github-org-intel/render_report.py`
    44|
    45|Reference docs:
    46|
    47|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/privacy-and-confidence.md`
    48|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/orbi-stream-mapping.md`
    49|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/output-templates.md`
    50|- `workspace/docs/github-org-intel/metric-definitions.md`
    51|
    52|## Workflows
    53|
    54|### 1. Portfolio snapshot
    55|
    56|Use when the user wants org posture.
    57|
    58|```bash
    59|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py --org orbisoptimus --format markdown
    60|```
    61|
    62|Output contract:
    63|- scope / timestamp / auth context
    64|- active repos summary
    65|- stale / archived summary
    66|- backlog hotspots
    67|- branch hygiene notes
    68|- Orbi stream mapping
    69|- priority actions
    70|- confidence / limitation notes
    71|
    72|### 2. Repo / module deep dive
    73|
    74|Use when the user names a repo.
    75|
    76|```bash
    77|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/repo_deep_dive.py --repo orbisoptimus/orbi_app --format markdown
    78|```
    79|
    80|Output contract:
    81|- repo identity + default branch
    82|- recent activity summary
    83|- issue / PR backlog summary
    84|- module-performance view
    85|- risks / unknowns
    86|- recommended next actions
    87|- confidence / limitation notes
    88|
    89|### 3. Account / contributor view
    90|
    91|Use only when the user explicitly names a target account.
    92|
    93|```bash
    94|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/account_summary.py --org orbisoptimus --account bellman-move --format markdown
    95|```
    96|
    97|Output contract:
    98|- target account
    99|- visible evidence summary
   100|- repo / PR / issue / review signals
   101|- confidence level
   102|- visibility caveats
   103|- non-evaluative note
   104|- recommended follow-up or insufficient visibility
   105|
   106|### 4. Management action memo
   107|
   108|Use when the user wants decisions, not raw telemetry.
   109|
   110|```bash
   111|tmp=$(mktemp)
   112|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/org_overview.py --org orbisoptimus --format json > "$tmp"
   113|python3 workspace/bin/github-org-intel/render_report.py --kind action-memo --input "$tmp"
   114|rm -f "$tmp"
   115|```
   116|
   117|Output contract:
   118|- executive summary
   119|- stream-by-stream implications
   120|- top action items
   121|- confidence / limitation notes
   122|
   123|## Orbi-specific framing
   124|
   125|Prefer grouping repos into:
   126|- core platform / web ops
   127|- mobile
   128|- AI / assistant
   129|- legacy cleanup
   130|
   131|Do not stop at telemetry. Finish with:
   132|- what matters now
   133|- where backlog/risk is concentrated
   134|- what leadership should do next
   135|
   136|## Guardrails
   137|
   138|- Do not imply direct GitHub mutation authority in this workflow.
   139|- Do not turn GitHub activity into compensation/promotion judgments.
   140|- Treat visibility-limited people analysis as low-confidence or insufficient visibility.
   141|- For internal/private repo reporting, summarize before exposing unnecessary detail.
   142|", "total_lines": 141, "file_size": 4052, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Skill — trello-portfolio-observer
     2|
     3|## Role
     4|
     5|`trello-portfolio-observer` is the **top-level/default/user-facing Trello surface**.
     6|Use it for read-only oversight across Trello boards: portfolio health, cross-board
     7|dependencies, stale/low-relevance work, bottlenecks, and executive summaries.
     8|
     9|## When to Use
    10|
    11|Route here by default for prompts like:
    12|
    13|- “What is happening across all Trello boards right now?”
    14|- “Give me a board health ranking.”
    15|- “Find cross-board dependencies and bottlenecks.”
    16|- “Which work looks stale or low relevance?”
    17|- “Summarize Trello work performance for leadership.”
    18|
    19|If the user wants only raw inventory collection, use `trello-board-scan` as an
    20|internal helper instead.
    21|
    22|## Evidence Stack
    23|
    24|1. Local Trello REST retrieval first
    25|2. Board/card/list/member evidence second
    26|3. Analytical synthesis third
    27|4. Executive compression last
    28|
    29|Preferred inputs via the local wrappers:
    30|
    31|- `./scripts/trello_boards.sh`
    32|- `./scripts/trello_board.sh --board <board-id>`
    33|- `./scripts/trello_rest.sh team-list`
    34|- `./scripts/trello_board_members.sh --board <board-id>`
    35|- `./scripts/trello_rest.sh rest-get '/boards/<board-id>/cards?fields=id,name,idList,closed,dateLastActivity'`
    36|
    37|## Workflow
    38|
    39|1. Reframe the prompt as a Trello portfolio question.
    40|2. Pull the minimum board/team/member/card evidence needed to answer credibly.
    41|3. Expand into dependency / stale-work / relevance / bottleneck analysis.
    42|4. Separate hard facts from inference.
    43|5. Produce the requested portfolio brief, ranking, or executive summary.
    44|6. Pull in subordinate Trello skills only when a narrower lane needs deeper work.
    45|
    46|## Output Contract
    47|
    48|Default structure:
    49|
    50|1. **Portfolio Snapshot** — what exists, what moved, and what stands out
    51|2. **Board Health Readout** — strongest and weakest boards with why
    52|3. **Dependency / Bottleneck Analysis** — cross-board coupling and blockage risk
    53|4. **Relevance / Priority / Staleness Notes** — work that looks misaligned or stale
    54|5. **Executive Summary** — short leadership-ready readout
    55|6. **Evidence vs Inference** — what is directly supported vs analytically inferred
    56|
    57|## Guardrails
    58|
    59|- Trello is strictly read-only in this workspace.
    60|- Local credential path is `workspace/creds/.trello.creds` only. Do not use `workspace/creds/trello.json`.
    61|- Do not imply card/list/comment/member/due-date/move/archive/automation authority.
    62|- Hard facts must be supportable from retrieved evidence.
    63|- Strong inference is allowed, but label it as inference.
    64|- Keep Trello as a separate skill family from Orbi.
    65|", "total_lines": 64, "file_size": 2550, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Company Wiki Schema
     2|
     3|## Canonical root
     4|- `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/company-wiki`
     5|
     6|## Authority
     7|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
     8|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
     9|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
    10|
    11|## Layout
    12|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
    13|- `entities/` — durable subjects
    14|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
    15|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
    16|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
    17|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
    18|
    19|## Classification fallback
    20|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.

{"content": "     1|#!/bin/zsh
     2|set -euo pipefail
     3|
     4|script_dir=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd)
     5|source "$script_dir/trello_rest_lib.sh"
     6|
     7|usage() {
     8|  cat <<'EOF'
     9|Usage:
    10|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh member-me
    11|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh board-list
    12|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh board-get --board <id>
    13|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh member-list --board <id>
    14|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh team-list
    15|  ./scripts/trello_rest.sh rest-get <allowed-path>
    16|
    17|Notes:
    18|  - Read-only only.
    19|  - Allowed custom paths are limited to /members/*, /boards/*, and /organizations/*
    20|  - Credentials load from workspace/creds/.trello.creds first, then workspace/creds/trello.json.
    21|EOF
    22|}
    23|
    24|[[ $# -gt 0 ]] || { usage; exit 1; }
    25|
    26|http_method="GET"
    27|if [[ "${1:-}" == "--method" ]]; then
    28|  shift
    29|  [[ $# -gt 0 ]] || trello_die "Missing value for --method"
    30|  http_method="${1:u}"
    31|  shift
    32|fi
    33|
    34|[[ "$http_method" == "GET" ]] || trello_die "Blocked Trello method '$http_method': this workspace is read-only."
    35|
    36|[[ $# -gt 0 ]] || { usage; exit 1; }
    37|
    38|command_name="$1"
    39|shift
    40|
    41|board_id=""
    42|
    43|while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    44|  case "$1" in
    45|    --board)
    46|      shift
    47|      [[ $# -gt 0 ]] || trello_die "Missing value for --board"
    48|      board_id="$1"
    49|      ;;
    50|    -h|--help)
    51|      usage
    52|      exit 0
    53|      ;;
    54|    *)
    55|      if [[ -z "$board_id" && "$command_name" == "rest-get" ]]; then
    56|        break
    57|      fi
    58|      trello_die "Unknown argument: $1"
    59|      ;;
    60|  esac
    61|  shift
    62|done
    63|
    64|case "$command_name" in
    65|  member-me)
    66|    trello_get "/members/me"
    67|    ;;
    68|  board-list)
    69|    trello_get "/members/me/boards?fields=name,id,closed,dateLastActivity,url"
    70|    ;;
    71|  board-get)
    72|    [[ -n "$board_id" ]] || trello_die "board-get requires --board <id>"
    73|    trello_get "/boards/${board_id}?fields=name,id,desc,closed,dateLastActivity,url,idOrganization"
    74|    ;;
    75|  board-cards)
    76|    [[ -n "$board_id" ]] || trello_die "board-cards requires --board <id>"
    77|    trello_get "/boards/${board_id}/cards?fields=id,name,idList,closed,dateLastActivity,url"
    78|    ;;
    79|  member-list)
    80|    [[ -n "$board_id" ]] || trello_die "member-list requires --board <id>"
    81|    trello_get "/boards/${board_id}/members?fields=id,fullName,username"
    82|    ;;
    83|  team-list)
    84|    trello_get "/members/me/organizations?fields=id,name,displayName,url"
    85|    ;;
    86|  rest-get)
    87|    [[ $# -gt 0 ]] || trello_die "rest-get requires an allowed path like /members/me/boards"
    88|    trello_get "$1"
    89|    ;;

{"code":"from hermes_tools import read_file, search_files
import re, json, os, glob
from datetime import datetime

# gather markdown files in company-wiki
files = search_files(pattern='*.md', target='files', path='workspace/company-wiki', limit=500)['files']
summary = {'company_wiki_md_count': len(files)}

issues = {
    'type_entitie_files': [],
    'absolute_zeroclaw_refs': [],
    'updated_timestamps': [],
}
for path in files:
    data = read_file(path=path, offset=1, limit=120)
    text = data['content']
    if 'type: entitie' in text:
        issues['type_entitie_files'].append(path)
    if '/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw' in text:
        issues['absolute_zeroclaw_refs'].append(path)
    m = re.search(r'updated:\s*([0-9T:\-]+Z)', text)
    if m:
        issues['updated_timestamps'].append((path, m.group(1)))

# sort by timestamp
issues['updated_timestamps'].sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
oldest = issues['updated_timestamps'][:5]
newest = issues['updated_timestamps'][-5:]

# inspect top-level sections counts
section_counts = {}
for section in ['entities','concepts','comparisons','queries']:
    sf = search_files(pattern='*.md', target='files', path=f'workspace/company-wiki/{section}', limit=500)
    section_counts[section] = sf['total_count']

# docs/skills references
ref_search = search_files(pattern='/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw|ZeroClaw', target='content', path='workspace', file_glob='*.md', limit=500, output_mode='count')
print(json.dumps({
    'section_counts': section_counts,
    'type_entitie_count': len(issues['type_entitie_files']),
    'type_entitie_files': issues['type_entitie_files'],
    'absolute_zeroclaw_ref_count_in_company_wiki': len(issues['absolute_zeroclaw_refs']),
    'oldest_updated_pages': oldest,
    'newest_updated_pages': newest,
    'md_ref_counts': ref_search['counts'][:20]
}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))"}

{"code":"from hermes_tools import search_files, read_file
import re, json
files = search_files(pattern='trello-board-*.md', target='files', path='workspace/company-wiki/entities', limit=50)['files']
out=[]
for path in files:
    text=read_file(path=path, offset=1, limit=120)['content']
    title=re.search(r'title: (.+)', text)
    last=re.search(r'Last activity: ([0-9T:\-.]+Z)', text)
    cards=re.search(r'Visible cards: (\d+)', text)
    members=re.search(r'Visible members: (\d+)', text)
    out.append({
        'path': path,
        'title': title.group(1) if title else None,
        'last_activity': last.group(1) if last else None,
        'visible_cards': int(cards.group(1)) if cards else None,
        'visible_members': int(members.group(1)) if members else None,
    })
print(json.dumps(sorted(out, key=lambda x: x['last_activity'] or ''), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))"}

# 2026-04-13 company wiki realignment and urgent actions

## Situation
현재 canonical company wiki는 살아는 있지만, **리더십이 바로 읽는 운영 위키**라기보다 **수집 결과를 나열한 저장소**에 가깝다.

근거:
- 현재 인덱스는 총 14페이지이며 `entities 11 / concepts 3 / comparisons 0 / queries 0` 구조다.
- 최신 refresh는 `2026-04-13T09:19:42Z`, build는 `2026-04-13T09:20:09Z`에 성공했다.
- Slack 쪽은 최신 refresh에서 public channels 7개 join 성공, history capture 7/7 성공이다.
- Trello 쪽은 open boards 4개가 반영돼 있다.
- 그런데 canonical surface 안에는 **GitHub 운영면이 아직 없다.** 반면 운영 라우팅 문서는 대표 질문에 대해 `company-wiki + GitHub + Trello` 결합을 사실상 표준으로 잡고 있다.

## Diagnosis
### 1) 정보구조가 운영 우선순위 기준으로 정렬돼 있지 않다
첫 화면이 사람/보드/채널 엔티티 나열 중심이다. 대표나 운영 리드가 보고 싶은 건
1. 지금 뭐가 움직이는지
2. 뭐가 막히는지
3. 오늘 당장 뭘 해야 하는지
인데, 그 레이어가 비어 있다.

### 2) Slack 파트는 수집 성공 대비 요약 승격이 안 됐다
최신 manifest는 Slack history capture 성공(7/7)을 말하는데, public-channel bootstrap 계열 페이지 8개는 아직 `updated: 2026-04-13T05:07:53Z`에 머물러 있다. 즉 **수집은 더 앞서가는데 위키 서술이 못 따라오고 있다.**

### 3) 위키 hygiene가 아직 migration-era 흔적을 품고 있다
- canonical pages 16개에 `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw` 절대경로가 남아 있다.
- entity pages 11개가 frontmatter에 `type: entitie` 오타를 가진다.
- log도 절대경로 기반 기록이 많다.
이건 검색/후속 자동화/이식성에서 계속 발목 잡는다.

### 4) Trello는 운영 신호가 보이는데 우선순위 해석 레이어가 비어 있다
현재 보이는 보드 상태:
- `Dev`: 40 cards, last activity `2026-04-13T05:21:49Z`
- `Design`: 258 cards, last activity `2026-04-07T01:03:03Z`
- `공지`: 19 cards, last activity `2026-04-12T22:02:46Z`
- `잡동사니_아무거나`: 17 cards, last activity `2025-11-17T01:25:00Z`

의미는 명확하다:
- Dev는 지금 움직이는 실무판이다.
- Design은 카드량 대비 최근 활동이 약해서 **백로그 압력** 가능성이 높다.
- 잡동사니는 사실상 **정리/아카이브 후보**다.
하지만 위키는 아직 이걸 경영 판단으로 압축하지 않는다.

### 5) “모든 소스” 기준으로는 아직 GitHub가 빠져 있다
GitHub live snapshot에서 최근 업데이트 repo 상위는 다음 흐름을 보여준다.
- `orbisoptimus/orbi-oracle-bot`
- `orbisoptimus/ai-math`
- `orbisoptimus/tutor`
- `orbisoptimus/orbi-viewer-mcp`
- `orbisoptimus/spica`

즉 현재 회사 실행축은 적어도
- AI/assistant
- tutor/education
- core product/app
으로 보이는데, canonical wiki는 아직 Slack+Trello까지만 묶는다.

## Recommended reordering
canonical wiki 첫 화면과 읽는 순서를 아래처럼 바꾸는 게 맞다.

### A. Executive layer
1. `queries/this-week-priorities.md`
2. `queries/blocked-or-stale-work.md`
3. `queries/company-wiki-realignment-and-actions.md`

### B. Stream layer
1. AI / assistant
2. Growth / ads
3. Product / tutor
4. Release / deploy

### C. Source layer
1. GitHub execution
2. Trello planning
3. Slack operating signal

### D. Raw/entity layer
- channels
- boards
- reference concepts

핵심은 **엔티티 중심 → 의사결정 중심** 전환이다.

## Urgent action items
### P0
1. **GitHub를 canonical company wiki에 합류시켜라.**  
   지금 구조는 “company wiki”라고 부르지만 실제론 Slack+Trello 위키다. 대표 관점 운영 위키라면 GitHub execution surface가 들어와야 한다.
2. **Slack latest refresh를 queries/action memo로 승격해라.**  
   7개 채널 history를 읽고도 action page가 0개인 상태는 낭비다.
3. **generator hygiene 바로 잡아라.**  
   `type: entitie` 오타, 절대경로 노출, stale updated timestamps를 이번 사이클에서 정리해야 한다.

### P1
4. **Design board를 backlog-pressure board로 별도 추적해라.**  
   카드 258장 대비 최근 활동이 약하다. owner/active lists/stale cards 기준으로 한번 더 압축해야 한다.
5. **`잡동사니_아무거나`는 archive-or-cleanup 판정 내려라.**  
   2025-11-17 이후 정지면 운영 위키 첫 화면에 남겨둘 자산이 아니다.
6. **`#서비스-배포-알림`을 운영 핵심 채널로 승격해라.**  
   22명 규모고 release/deploy 성격이 명확하다. 운영 사고/배포 cadence 시그널의 우선 채널이다.

### P2
7. **comparisons/queries를 자동 생성하게 바꿔라.**  
   지금 `comparisons 0 / queries 0`는 위키가 생각을 안 하고 있다는 뜻이다.
8. **index를 source-first가 아니라 action-first로 재작성해라.**

## Concrete operating view
지금 위키 기준으로 당장 보는 게 맞는 건 세 가지다.
- **실행축:** GitHub 상단 활성 repo 흐름은 AI/assistant + tutor/product 쪽이다.
- **운영축:** Slack은 deploy, ads, AI, tutor 관련 public signal이 잡힌다.
- **계획축:** Trello는 Dev가 현재 실행판, Design은 과적 가능성, 잡동사니는 정리 후보다.

## Bottom line
이 위키는 **수집 체계는 붙었고, 경영 위키로의 마지막 20%가 비어 있다.**
지금 급한 건 데이터를 더 모으는 게 아니라,
1. GitHub를 canonical surface에 넣고,
2. Slack/Trello 결과를 action memo로 승격하고,
3. stale/hygiene 문제를 치우는 것이다.
"}

핵심 판단만 바로 말하면:

### 지금 위키 상태
- 살아있는 위키는 맞는데, **운영 의사결정 위키**보다 **수집 결과 보관함**에 가깝다.
- 기존 인덱스는 `entities 11 / concepts 3 / comparisons 0 / queries 0`였고, 첫 화면이 엔티티 나열 중심이었다.
- 그래서 내가 **Queries를 맨 위로 올리고**, 대표/운영용 재정렬 메모를 첫 진입 문서로 추가했다.

### 급한 액션아이템
**P0**
1. **GitHub를 canonical wiki에 편입**
   - 지금 company-wiki는 실질적으로 Slack+Trello 위키다.
   - 그런데 운영 라우팅은 이미 `company-wiki + GitHub + Trello` 조합을 표준처럼 쓰고 있다.
   - 즉 “모든 소스” 기준으로는 아직 반쪽짜리다.

2. **Slack 수집 결과를 action memo로 승격**
   - 최신 refresh 기준 public channel 7개 join 성공, history 7/7 성공.
   - 그런데 위키는 아직 action page가 거의 없다.
   - 수집은 됐는데 판단 레이어가 비어 있다.

3. **위키 hygiene 정리**
   - canonical pages 다수에 `/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw` 절대경로 흔적 남아 있음
   - entity pages 11개 frontmatter가 `type: entitie` 오타
   - Slack bootstrap 페이지 8개는 최신 refresh보다 timestamp가 뒤처져 있음

**P1**
4. **Design 보드 별도 압축**
   - `Design`: 카드 258, 최근 활동 `2026-04-07`
   - 양 대비 활동이 약해서 backlog pressure 가능성 큼

5. **`잡동사니_아무거나` 정리/아카이브 판정**
   - 최근 활동 `2025-11-17`
   - 위키 전면에 둘 대상은 아님

6. **`#서비스-배포-알림` 운영 핵심 채널로 승격**
   - 멤버 22명, deploy/release 성격이 명확
   - 운영 리스크/배포 cadence 감시 채널로 우선순위 높음

### 소스별 한 줄 요약
- **Slack:** deploy / ads / AI / tutor 축이 보임
- **Trello:** Dev는 현재 실행판, Design은 과적 가능성, 잡동사니는 정리 후보
- **GitHub:** 최근 활성 상위 repo가 `orbi-oracle-bot`, `ai-math`, `tutor`, `orbi-viewer-mcp`, `spica`라서 AI/assistant + tutor/product 축이 강함

한 줄 결론:
**지금 필요한 건 데이터를 더 모으는 게 아니라, GitHub를 붙이고, Slack/Trello 결과를 실행 문서로 승격하고, migration-era 흔적을 치우는 것**이다.

     4|import argparse

     9|from pathlib import Path

    11|
    12|import slack_snapshot

    14|from _common import (
    15|    COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT,
    16|    LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    17|    CompanyWikiError,
    18|    append_contract_log,
    19|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root,
    20|    company_wiki_paths,
    21|    ensure_contract_ready,
    22|    now_iso,
    23|    write_json,
    24|)
    25|
    26|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
    27|TMP_ROOT = ROOT / 'workspace' / 'tmp'
    28|
    29|
    30|def copy_tree_contents(src: Path, dest: Path) -> None:
    31|    dest.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    32|    for item in src.iterdir():
    33|        target = dest / item.name
    34|        if target.exists():
    35|            continue
    36|        if item.is_dir():
    37|            shutil.copytree(item, target)
    38|        else:
    39|            shutil.copy2(item, target)
    40|
    41|
    42|def import_legacy_raw_history(raw_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    43|    imported_root = raw_root / 'imports' / 'legacy-workspace-raw-company-wiki'
    44|    result = {
    45|        'legacy_source': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    46|        'import_root': str(imported_root),
    47|        'imported': False,
    48|        'copied_item_count': 0,
    49|        'cutover_map': str(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json'),
    50|        'legacy_exists': LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists(),
    51|    }
    52|    if not LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT.exists():
    53|        return result
    54|
    55|    copy_tree_contents(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT, imported_root)
    56|    result['imported'] = True
    57|    result['copied_item_count'] = sum(1 for _ in imported_root.rglob('*'))
    58|    cutover_map = {
    59|        'cutover_at': now_iso(),
    60|        'policy': {
    61|            'historical_artifacts_are_immutable': True,
    62|            'old_root_paths_preserved_in_historical_artifacts': True,
    63|            'silent_path_rewrite_for_raw_evidence': False,
    64|            'canonical_provenance_exposed_via_cutover_mapping': True,
    65|        },
    66|        'legacy_source_root': str(LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT),
    67|        'canonical_import_root': str(imported_root),
    68|    }
    69|    write_json(raw_root / 'cutover-map.json', cutover_map)
    70|    append_contract_log(
    71|        'company-wiki',
    72|        'raw-import',
    73|        root=raw_root.parent,
    74|        legacy_source=LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT,
    75|        import_root=imported_root,
    76|    )
    77|    return result
    78|
    79|
    80|def main() -> None:
    81|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Refresh the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from Slack and Trello snapshots.')
    82|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT))
    83|    parser.add_argument('--slack-team', default='')
    84|    parser.add_argument('--archive-mode', choices=['archive', 'incremental'], default='incremental')
    85|    parser.add_argument('--slack-history-limit', type=int, default=0, help='Per-channel conversations.history page size. Uses mode defaults when omitted.')
    86|    parser.add_argument('--slack-history-chunk-size', type=int, default=100, help='Persist conversations.history payloads in chunked raw item sizes.')
    87|    parser.add_argument('--slack-channel-limit', type=int, default=0, help='Max candidate channels to process this run. Uses mode defaults when omitted.')
    88|    args = parser.parse_args()
    89|
    90|    root = Path(args.root)
    91|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
    92|    ensure_contract_ready('company-wiki', root=root)
    93|    paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
    94|    generated_at = now_iso()
    95|    TMP_ROOT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    96|
    97|    import_result = import_legacy_raw_history(paths['raw'])
    98|
    99|    run_manifest: dict[str, Any] = {
   100|        'generated_at': generated_at,
   101|        'canonical_root': str(paths['root']),
   102|        'sources': {},
   103|        'import': import_result,
   104|        'build_root': str(paths['root']),
   105|    }
   106|    all_entries: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   107|
   108|    try:
   109|        slack_result = slack_snapshot.capture(
   110|            raw_root=paths['raw'],
   111|            slack_team=args.slack_team,
   112|            archive_mode=args.archive_mode,
   113|            history_limit=(args.slack_history_limit or None),
   114|            chunk_size=max(1, args.slack_history_chunk_size),
   115|            channel_limit=(args.slack_channel_limit or None),
   116|        )
   117|        all_entries.extend(slack_result['entries'])
   118|        run_manifest['sources']['slack'] = {
   119|            'status': slack_result['status'],
   120|            'manifest': slack_result['manifest'],
   121|            'archive_mode': args.archive_mode,
   122|            'history_summary': slack_result.get('history_summary', {}),
   123|        }
   124|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   125|        run_manifest['sources']['slack'] = {'status': 'blocked', 'blocker': str(exc)}
   126|
   127|    try:
   128|        trello_result = trello_snapshot.capture(repo_root=ROOT, raw_root=paths['raw'])
   129|        all_entries.extend(trello_result['entries'])
   130|        run_manifest['sources']['trello'] = {'status': trello_result['status'], 'manifest': trello_result['manifest']}
   131|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   132|        run_manifest['sources']['trello'] = {'status': 'blocked', 'blocker': str(exc)}
   133|
   134|    entries_path = paths['build_normalized'] / f'entries-{generated_at.replace(":", "").replace("-", "")}.json'
   135|    write_json(entries_path, {'generated_at': generated_at, 'entries': all_entries})
   136|
   137|    build_proc = subprocess.run(
   138|        [
   139|            sys.executable,
   140|            str(ROOT / 'workspace/bin/company-wiki/wiki_build.py'),
   141|            '--input',
   142|            str(entries_path),
   143|            '--root',
   144|            str(paths['root']),
   145|        ],
   146|        cwd=ROOT,
   147|        capture_output=True,
   148|        text=True,
   149|    )
   150|    if build_proc.returncode != 0:
   151|        raise SystemExit((build_proc.stderr or build_proc.stdout).strip() or 'company wiki build failed')
   152|    build_output = json.loads(build_proc.stdout)
   153|
   154|    run_manifest['build'] = build_output
   155|    run_manifest['entry_count'] = len(all_entries)
   156|    run_manifest['entries_path'] = str(entries_path)
   157|    run_manifest_path = paths['raw'] / 'run-manifests' / f'{generated_at.replace(":", "").replace("-", "")}.json'
   158|    write_json(run_manifest_path, run_manifest)
   159|    append_contract_log(
   160|        'company-wiki',
   161|        'refresh',
   162|        root=paths['root'],
   163|        run_manifest=run_manifest_path,
   164|        canonical_root=paths['root'],
   165|        entries_path=entries_path,
   166|        entry_count=len(all_entries),
   167|    )
   168|    print(
   169|        json.dumps(
   170|            {
   171|                'ok': True,
   172|                'generated_at': generated_at,
   173|                'run_manifest': str(run_manifest_path),
   174|                'sources': run_manifest['sources'],
   175|                'build': build_output,
   176|                'canonical_root': str(paths['root']),
   177|                'import': import_result,
   178|            },
   179|            ensure_ascii=False,
   180|            indent=2,
   181|        )
   182|    )
   183|
   184|
   185|if __name__ == '__main__':
   186|    try:
   187|        main()
   188|    except CompanyWikiError as exc:
   189|        raise SystemExit(str(exc))

{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|from __future__ import annotations
     3|
     4|import argparse
     5|import json
     6|import shutil
     7|from collections import defaultdict
     8|from pathlib import Path
     9|from typing import Any
    10|
    11|from _common import (
    12|    COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT,
    13|    CompanyWikiError,
    14|    append_contract_log,
    15|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root,
    16|    compact_unique,
    17|    company_wiki_paths,
    18|    ensure_contract_ready,
    19|    ensure_entry,
    20|    now_iso,
    21|    read_json,
    22|    slugify,
    23|    write_json,
    24|    write_text,
    25|)
    26|
    27|SECTION_DIRS = ('entities', 'concepts', 'comparisons', 'queries')
    28|
    29|
    30|def load_entries(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    31|    payload = read_json(path)
    32|    entries = payload.get('entries') if isinstance(payload, dict) else payload
    33|    if not isinstance(entries, list):
    34|        raise CompanyWikiError('Expected a top-level JSON array or an object with an entries array.')
    35|    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    36|    for item in entries:
    37|        if not isinstance(item, dict):
    38|            raise CompanyWikiError('Every entry must be a JSON object.')
    39|        ensure_entry(item)
    40|        out.append(item)
    41|    return out
    42|
    43|
    44|def classify_entry(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    45|    topic = str(entry.get('topic') or '').lower()
    46|    title = str(entry.get('title') or '').lower()
    47|    source_type = str(entry.get('source_type') or '').lower()
    48|    text = ' '.join([topic, title])
    49|    if 'comparison' in text or 'vs' in text:
    50|        return 'comparisons'
    51|    if source_type == 'trello' and topic.startswith('trello-board-'):
    52|        return 'entities'
    53|    if source_type == 'slack' and topic.startswith('public-channel-'):
    54|        return 'entities'
    55|    if 'overview' in text or topic.endswith('-overview') or 'history capture' in text:
    56|        return 'concepts'
    57|    return 'queries'
    58|
    59|
    60|def merge_entries(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    61|    grouped: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    62|    buckets: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
    63|    for entry in entries:
    64|        key = slugify(str(entry.get('topic') or entry.get('title')))
    65|        buckets[key].append(entry)
    66|
    67|    for key, group in buckets.items():
    68|        title = str(group[0].get('title') or group[0].get('topic') or key)
    69|        bucket = group[0]
    70|        section = classify_entry(bucket)
    71|        grouped[key] = {
    72|            'slug': key,
    73|            'title': title,
    74|            'section': section,
    75|            'summary': ' '.join(compact_unique(str(item.get('summary', '')) for item in group)),
    76|            'facts': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('facts', []) or []),
    77|            'decisions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('decisions', []) or []),
    78|            'actions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('actions', []) or []),
    79|            'open_questions': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('open_questions', []) or []),
    80|            'entities': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('entities', []) or []),
    81|            'channel_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('channel_refs', []) or []),
    82|            'source_refs': compact_unique(x for item in group for x in item.get('source_refs', []) or []),
    83|            'source_types': compact_unique(str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('source_type', '')).strip()),
    84|            'access_scopes': compact_unique(str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('access_scope', '')).strip()),
    85|            'raw_manifest_refs': compact_unique(str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('raw_manifest_ref', '')).strip()),
    86|            'time_ranges': compact_unique(str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('time_range', '')).strip()),
    87|            'confidences': compact_unique(str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('confidence', '')).strip()),
    88|            'updated_at': compact_unique(str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip() for item in group if str(item.get('updated_at', '')).strip())[-1:] or [now_iso()],
    89|            'classification_rationale': f"classified as {section} from topic/title/source_type heuristics",
    90|        }
    91|    return grouped
    92|
    93|
    94|def render_page(page: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    95|    frontmatter = [
    96|        '---',
    97|        f"title: {page['title']}",
    98|        f"updated: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
    99|        f"type: {page['section'][:-1] if page['section'].endswith('s') else page['section']}",
   100|        f"sources: [{', '.join(page['raw_manifest_refs'])}]",
   101|        '---',
   102|        '',
   103|    ]
   104|    lines = [
   105|        f"# {page['title']}",
   106|        '',
   107|        f"- Updated at: {page['updated_at'][0]}",
   108|        f"- Classification: {page['section']}",
   109|        f"- Confidence: {', '.join(page['confidences']) or 'unspecified'}",
   110|        f"- Source types: {', '.join(page['source_types']) or 'unspecified'}",
   111|        f"- Access scopes: {', '.join(page['access_scopes']) or 'unspecified'}",
   112|        f"- Provenance: {', '.join(page['raw_manifest_refs']) or 'none recorded'}",
   113|        '',
   114|        '## Summary',
   115|        page['summary'],
   116|        '',
   117|        '## Classification note',
   118|        f"- {page['classification_rationale']}",
   119|    ]
   120|    sections = [
   121|        ('Key facts', page['facts']),
   122|        ('Decisions', page['decisions']),
   123|        ('Actions', page['actions']),
   124|        ('Open questions', page['open_questions']),
   125|        ('Entities', page['entities']),
   126|        ('Channel references', page['channel_refs']),
   127|        ('Source references', page['source_refs']),
   128|        ('Raw manifest references', page['raw_manifest_refs']),
   129|    ]
   130|    for title, items in sections:
   131|        lines += ['', f'## {title}']
   132|        lines.extend(f'- {item}' for item in items) if items else lines.append('- none recorded')
   133|    lines += [
   134|        '',
   135|        '## Curation note',
   136|        '- This page was built from structured summaries only.',
   137|        '- Historical imported raw artifacts remain immutable even when they still contain old-root path strings.',
   138|    ]
   139|    return '\n'.join(frontmatter + lines) + '\n'
   140|
   141|
   142|def render_schema(root: Path) -> str:
   143|    return f"""# Company Wiki Schema
   144|
   145|## Canonical root
   146|- `{root}`
   147|
   148|## Authority
   149|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
   150|- `SCHEMA.md`, `index.md`, and `log.md` here are authoritative.
   151|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
   152|
   153|## Layout
   154|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots, imported history, and manifests
   155|- `entities/` — durable subjects
   156|- `concepts/` — durable operating knowledge
   157|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
   158|- `queries/` — retained syntheses and fallback classification bucket
   159|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized inputs, cutover metadata
   160|
   161|## Classification fallback
   162|- If an entry does not fit `entities/`, `concepts/`, or `comparisons/`, it lands in `queries/`.
   163|"""
   164|
   165|
   166|def render_index(pages_by_section: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]) -> str:
   167|    total = sum(len(items) for items in pages_by_section.values())
   168|    lines = [
   169|        '# Company Wiki Index',
   170|        '',
   171|        f'- Built at: {now_iso()}',
   172|        f'- Page count: {total}',
   173|    ]
   174|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   175|        lines += ['', f'## {section.title()}']
   176|        items = sorted(pages_by_section.get(section, []), key=lambda item: item['title'].lower())
   177|        if not items:
   178|            lines.append('- none')
   179|            continue
   180|        for page in items:
   181|            lines.append(f"- [{page['title']}]({section}/{page['slug']}.md) — updated {page['updated_at']}")
   182|    return '\n'.join(lines) + '\n'
   183|
   184|
   185|def clean_section_dirs(root_paths: dict[str, Path]) -> None:
   186|    for section in SECTION_DIRS:
   187|        section_dir = root_paths[section]
   188|        if section_dir.exists():
   189|            shutil.rmtree(section_dir)
   190|        section_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   191|
   192|
   193|def main() -> None:
   194|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Build the canonical Hermes-style company wiki from structured summary JSON.')
   195|    parser.add_argument('--input', required=True, help='Path to structured summary JSON.')
   196|    parser.add_argument('--root', default=str(COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT), help='Canonical company wiki root.')
   197|    args = parser.parse_args()
   198|
   199|    root = Path(args.root)
   200|    bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
   201|    ensure_contract_ready('company-wiki', root=root)
   202|    root_paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   203|    entries = load_entries(Path(args.input))
   204|    merged = merge_entries(entries)
   205|    clean_section_dirs(root_paths)
   206|
   207|    manifest_pages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

     3|import hashlib
     4|import json
     5|import os

     9|from typing import Any, Iterable
    10|
    11|UTC = timezone.utc
    12|REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
    13|DOCS_ROOT_ENV = 'ZEROCLAW_LLM_WIKI_DOCS_ROOT'
    14|COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / 'workspace' / 'company-wiki'
    15|LEGACY_COMPANY_RAW_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / 'workspace' / 'raw' / 'company-wiki'
    16|SHARED_CONTRACT_FILES = (
    17|    Path('llm-wiki/index.md'),
    18|    Path('llm-wiki/log.md'),
    19|    Path('llm-wiki/taxonomy.md'),
    20|)
    21|LANE_SCHEMA_FILES = {
    22|    'company-wiki': Path('company-wiki/schema.md'),
    23|    'slack-org-wiki': Path('slack-org-wiki/llm-wiki-schema.md'),
    24|}
    25|FORBIDDEN_RAW_KEYS = {
    26|    'body',
    27|    'content',

    29|    'message',
    30|    'message_body',
    31|    'message_content',

    41|    'text',
    42|    'transcript',
    43|    'transcript_excerpt',
    44|    'transcript_path',
    45|}
    46|ALLOWED_ENTRY_KEYS = {
    47|    'actions',
    48|    'access_scope',
    49|    'channel_refs',
    50|    'confidence',
    51|    'decisions',
    52|    'entities',
    53|    'facts',
    54|    'open_questions',
    55|    'raw_manifest_ref',
    56|    'source_refs',
    57|    'source_type',
    58|    'summary',
    59|    'time_range',
    60|    'title',
    61|    'topic',
    62|    'updated_at',
    63|}
    64|STRING_LIST_FIELDS = {
    65|    'actions',
    66|    'channel_refs',
    67|    'decisions',
    68|    'entities',
    69|    'facts',
    70|    'open_questions',
    71|    'source_refs',
    72|}
    73|STRING_FIELDS = {
    74|    'access_scope',
    75|    'confidence',
    76|    'raw_manifest_ref',
    77|    'source_type',
    78|    'summary',
    79|    'time_range',
    80|    'title',
    81|    'topic',
    82|    'updated_at',
    83|}
    84|
    85|
    86|class CompanyWikiError(RuntimeError):
    87|    pass
    88|
    89|
    90|def now_iso() -> str:
    91|    return datetime.now(UTC).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace('+00:00', 'Z')
    92|
    93|
    94|def slugify(value: str) -> str:
    95|    value = value.strip().lower()
    96|    value = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', value)
    97|    return value.strip('-') or 'untitled'
    98|
    99|
   100|def compact_unique(items: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
   101|    seen: set[str] = set()
   102|    out: list[str] = []
   103|    for item in items:
   104|        value = str(item).strip()
   105|        if not value or value in seen:
   106|            continue
   107|        seen.add(value)
   108|        out.append(value)
   109|    return out
   110|
   111|
   112|def normalize_key(value: str) -> str:
   113|    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '', str(value).lower())
   114|
   115|
   116|def read_json(path: Path) -> Any:
   117|    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
   118|
   119|
   120|def write_json(path: Path, payload: Any) -> None:
   121|    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   122|    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')
   123|
   124|
   125|def write_text(path: Path, text: str) -> None:
   126|    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   127|    path.write_text(text, encoding='utf-8')
   128|
   129|
   130|def company_wiki_paths(root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Path]:
   131|    canonical_root = (root or COMPANY_WIKI_ROOT).expanduser()
   132|    return {
   133|        'root': canonical_root,
   134|        'schema': canonical_root / 'SCHEMA.md',
   135|        'index': canonical_root / 'index.md',
   136|        'log': canonical_root / 'log.md',
   137|        'raw': canonical_root / 'raw',
   138|        'entities': canonical_root / 'entities',
   139|        'concepts': canonical_root / 'concepts',
   140|        'comparisons': canonical_root / 'comparisons',
   141|        'queries': canonical_root / 'queries',
   142|        'build': canonical_root / '_build',
   143|        'build_normalized': canonical_root / '_build' / 'normalized',
   144|        'build_manifest': canonical_root / '_build' / 'manifest.json',
   145|        'cutover_map': canonical_root / 'raw' / 'cutover-map.json',
   146|    }
   147|
   148|
   149|def default_company_schema() -> str:
   150|    return """# Company Wiki Schema
   151|
   152|## Authority
   153|- Canonical root: `workspace/company-wiki/`
   154|- This root is the only supported company wiki surface.
   155|- `workspace/docs/llm-wiki/*` is reference-only and unsupported for active company-wiki flow.
   156|
   157|## Layout
   158|- `raw/` — immutable source snapshots and manifests
   159|- `entities/` — durable subjects (channels, boards, teams, systems)
   160|- `concepts/` — durable themes, workflows, operating patterns
   161|- `comparisons/` — side-by-side evaluations
   162|- `queries/` — retained synthesis pages and fallback classification bucket
   163|- `_build/` — generated manifests, normalized intermediates, and cutover metadata
   164|
   165|## Rules
   166|- Do not mutate imported historical raw artifacts in place.
   167|- Preserve provenance from derived pages back into canonical `raw/`.
   168|- When classification is ambiguous, default to `queries/`.
   169|"""
   170|
   171|
   172|def bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
   173|    paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   174|    for key in ('root', 'raw', 'entities', 'concepts', 'comparisons', 'queries', 'build', 'build_normalized'):
   175|        paths[key].mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   176|    if not paths['schema'].exists():
   177|        write_text(paths['schema'], default_company_schema())
   178|    if not paths['index'].exists():
   179|        write_text(paths['index'], '# Company Wiki Index\n\n- Built at: never\n- Page count: 0\n')
   180|    if not paths['log'].exists():
   181|        write_text(paths['log'], '# Company Wiki Log\n\n')
   182|    return {key: str(value) for key, value in paths.items()}
   183|
   184|
   185|def contract_docs_root() -> Path:
   186|    override = os.environ.get(DOCS_ROOT_ENV, '').strip()
   187|    return Path(override).expanduser() if override else REPO_ROOT / 'workspace' / 'docs'
   188|
   189|
   190|def contract_requirements(lane: str, root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Path]:
   191|    if lane == 'company-wiki':
   192|        paths = company_wiki_paths(root)
   193|        return {
   194|            'root': paths['root'],
   195|            'schema': paths['schema'],
   196|            'index': paths['index'],
   197|            'log': paths['log'],
   198|            'raw': paths['raw'],
   199|            'entities': paths['entities'],
   200|            'concepts': paths['concepts'],
   201|            'comparisons': paths['comparisons'],
   202|            'queries': paths['queries'],
   203|        }
   204|    docs_root = contract_docs_root()
   205|    if lane not in LANE_SCHEMA_FILES:
   206|        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Unsupported llm-wiki contract lane: {lane}')
   207|    return {
   208|        'docs_root': docs_root,
   209|        'shared_index': docs_root / SHARED_CONTRACT_FILES[0],
   210|        'shared_log': docs_root / SHARED_CONTRACT_FILES[1],
   211|        'shared_taxonomy': docs_root / SHARED_CONTRACT_FILES[2],
   212|        'lane_schema': docs_root / LANE_SCHEMA_FILES[lane],
   213|    }
   214|
   215|
   216|def ensure_contract_ready(lane: str, root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
   217|    if lane == 'company-wiki':
   218|        bootstrap_company_wiki_root(root)
   219|    requirements = contract_requirements(lane, root)
   220|    ignored_keys = {'docs_root'}
   221|    missing = [str(path) for key, path in requirements.items() if key not in ignored_keys and not path.exists()]
   222|    if missing:
   223|        raise CompanyWikiError(
   224|            f'llm-wiki contract missing for {lane}: {", ".join(missing)}.'
   225|        )
   226|    return {key: str(value) for key, value in requirements.items()}
   227|
   228|
   229|def append_contract_log(lane: str, event: str, *, root: Path | None = None, **details: Any) -> Path:
   230|    requirements = contract_requirements(lane, root)
   231|    log_key = 'log' if lane == 'company-wiki' else 'shared_log'
   232|    log_path = requirements[log_key]
   233|    log_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   234|    lines = [
   235|        '',
   236|        f'## {now_iso()} — {lane} {event}',
   237|        f'- lane: {lane}',
   238|        f'- event: {event}',
   239|    ]
   240|    for key, value in sorted(details.items()):
   241|        lines.append(f'- {key}: {value}')
   242|    with log_path.open('a', encoding='utf-8') as handle:
   243|        handle.write('\n'.join(lines) + '\n')
   244|    return log_path
   245|
   246|
   247|def _normalize_for_hash(payload: Any) -> Any:
   248|    volatile_keys = {'generated_at', 'updated_at', 'first_seen_at', 'last_seen_at'}
   249|    if isinstance(payload, dict):
   250|        return {
   251|            key: _normalize_for_hash(value)
   252|            for key, value in sorted(payload.items())
   253|            if key not in volatile_keys
   254|        }
   255|    if isinstance(payload, list):
   256|        return [_normalize_for_hash(item) for item in payload]
   257|    return payload
   258|
   259|
   260|def hash_payload(payload: Any) -> str:
   261|    canonical = json.dumps(_normalize_for_hash(payload), ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'))
   262|    return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
   263|
   264|
   265|def forbidden_keys(entry: Any, *, path: str = 'entry') -> list[str]:
   266|    normalized_forbidden = {normalize_key(key) for key in FORBIDDEN_RAW_KEYS}
   267|    out: list[str] = []
   268|    if isinstance(entry, dict):
   269|        for key, value in entry.items():
   270|            current_path = f'{path}.{key}'
   271|            if normalize_key(key) in normalized_forbidden:
   272|                out.append(current_path)
   273|            out.extend(forbidden_keys(value, path=current_path))
   274|    elif isinstance(entry, list):
   275|        for index, value in enumerate(entry):
   276|            out.extend(forbidden_keys(value, path=f'{path}[{index}]'))
   277|    return sorted(compact_unique(out))
   278|
   279|
   280|def ensure_string_field(entry: dict[str, Any], field: str) -> None:
   281|    value = entry.get(field)
   282|    if value is None:
   283|        return
   284|    if not isinstance(value, str):
   285|        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Field {field} must be a string.')
   286|
   287|
   288|def ensure_string_list_field(entry: dict[str, Any], field: str) -> None:
   289|    value = entry.get(field)
   290|    if value is None:
   291|        return
   292|    if not isinstance(value, list):
   293|        raise CompanyWikiError(f'Field {field} must be a list of strings.')
   294|    bad_indexes = [str(index) for index, item in enumerate(value) if not isinstance(item, str)]
   295|    if bad_indexes:
   296|        raise CompanyWikiError(
   297|            f'Field {field} must contain only strings; invalid items at indexes {", ".join(bad_indexes)}.'
   298|        )
   299|
   300|
   301|def ensure_entry(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   302|    bad_keys = forbidden_keys(entry)
   303|    if bad_keys:
   304|        title = entry.get('title') or entry.get('topic') or '<untitled>'
   305|        raise CompanyWikiError(
   306|            f'Entry {title!r} contains forbidden raw-content keys: {", ".join(bad_keys)}'
   307|        )
   308|    unsupported = sorted(str(key) for key in entry.keys() if key not in ALLOWED_ENTRY_KEYS)
   309|    if unsupported:
   310|        raise CompanyWikiError(
   311|            f'Unsupported keys in entry {entry.get("title") or entry.get("topic") or "<untitled>"!r}: '
   312|            + ', '.join(unsupported)
   313|        )
   314|    for field in STRING_FIELDS:
   315|        ensure_string_field(entry, field)
   316|    for field in STRING_LIST_FIELDS:
   317|        ensure_string_list_field(entry, field)
   318|    if not (entry.get('title') or entry.get('topic')):
   319|        raise CompanyWikiError('Each entry must define at least `title` or `topic`.')
   320|    if not isinstance(entry.get('summary'), str) or not entry['summary'].strip():
   321|        raise CompanyWikiError('Each entry must define a non-empty `summary`.')
